lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Ceril.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: That's Sir Ceril to you, you impudent scamp. Show a bit of respect to your betters. Now, shouldn't you be recovering my armour?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah yeah, I'm on it.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Good. I suggest you start with that cave south of the city, near the Clock Tower. That is where the cult is based.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello there. I hear you're after that cult who broke in the other night. It always gladdens me when civilians assist the law like this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just happy to be of help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Hello. How are you today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good thank you, and yourself?

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Very well, thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: Oh, hello. I'm afraid I'm not very presentable at the moment. I've been under incredible stress of late! It's ruining my complexion!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why? What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: It's those awful cultists! I just don't feel safe here anymore!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Mommy said you're here to kill all the nasty people that keep breaking in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something like that.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Can I watch?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No!

sections.title: Using any item on Claus' range

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Oi - I don't want people messing around with my range!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Sorry, can't stop to chat! You would be amazed at how many meals this family gets through daily!

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Hey! I don't remember saying you could use that raft!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Blooming, thieving cultists! Why don't they leave me alone? String them all up, that's what I say!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: It's those blooming cultists from the south! They keep breaking into my house!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have they taken much?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Well... no. They stole a suit of armour when they first broke in months ago, but they've been back four more times since then and taken nothing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. And you are?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: You don't know me? Why, I am Sir Ceril Carnillean! Mine is quite a famous bloodline, you know. We've played a vital role in the politics of Ardougne for many generations.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm [player name]. Maybe I can help you with these cultists?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Interesting... I suppose the authorities haven't been much help so far. Yes, very well. If you return the stolen armour, I will provide you with a modest cash reward!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, of course, I'd be happy to help.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: That's very noble of you! Keep that attitude up and one day you might have the honour of working full-time for someone like me.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Now, I caught a glimpse of the thieves leaving, but due to... uh... my cold, I was unable to give chase.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: What I do know is that they belong to some sort of cult. They seem to be based in a cave just south of the city, near the Clock Tower.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you know that?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: My old butler, Higson, once followed them there. Unfortunately, the next night he died in his sleep.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's awful!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: No, it's okay. Jones, a replacement from the Servants' Guild, arrived the next day. He's been great - makes an excellent broth!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... I'll see if I can find this cave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks. I've got other plans.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Well no wonder I'm the one with the big house and you're the one on the streets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You probably deserve it.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: And who are you to judge me? You look like a peasant anyway. I'm wasting my time talking to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem uptight. I'll leave you alone.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Yes, I doubt you could help anyway.

sections.title: Talking to Clivet again before joining

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: So, you've returned. I hope you're here because you've recognised the folly in serving the Carnilleans. Think about what you could achieve if you join us instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. Who are you?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: You can call me Clivet. What do you want, adventurer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know the Carnilleans?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Carnilleans? Don't think I've ever heard of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I know you're lying. I know all about your cult.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Is that so? In that case, I'd say you'd be wise to leave now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Unfortunately, I have a job to do. I'm not leaving until it's done.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Ah, I see. That two-faced snob has made you fall for his propaganda, eh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that supposed to mean?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: The Carnillean home does not belong to them. It was built long ago by the great Lord Hazeel, who once ruled over this entire region.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Many years ago, the Carnilleans led a rebellion against Lord Hazeel and all who followed him. Under the cover of darkness, they stormed his home in an angry mob, torturing and butchering everyone they found.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: The following morning, the Carnillean forefathers moved into the empty household and claimed it as their own. They have grown fat on the hard work of Lord Hazeel ever since.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... And what happened to this Hazeel?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: The foolish Carnilleans believed him dead. Unluckily for them, being an all-powerful Mahjarrat gave him access to powers and enchantments they knew nothing of.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: As they stormed his home, Lord Hazeel made preparations for his eventual return. We, his faithful followers, have been working to bring that about ever since.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What makes you think I care about the politics and histories of Ardougne?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: I could kill you right now and be done with it, but I don't think you're the fool that the Carnilleans believe you to be. Instead, I offer you a chance to join us.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Help us bring about the return of Lord Hazeel, and you will be well rewarded. However, should you continue to try and prevent his return, you will suffer the wrath of Zamorak.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Before I decide, I have some questions for you.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about Hazeel.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Lord Hazeel is one of the great Mahjarrat who once fought alongside Zamorak himself. He ruled this region for many years, until the cowardly Carnilleans rebelled against him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But he survived?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Of course! The Mahjarrat know magic that others can only dream of. Lord Hazeel used his abilities to ensure that one day he'd eventually return. That day will soon be upon us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did the Carnilleans rebel against Hazeel?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: The Carnilleans served the pathetic Saradomin. They considered it their duty to overthrow Hazeel due to his loyalties to Zamorak. They were fools, blinded by Saradominist propaganda!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Saradominist propaganda?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Yes! Something even more prevalent today. Too many people now are fooled into believing that Saradomin is a god of good, while Zamorak is evil. Those lies couldn't be further from the truth!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you keep breaking into the Carnillean Mansion?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: There is something we need inside. I will say no more unless you agree to join us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the Mahjarrat?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Surely you have heard of the Mahjarrat? Their race is the ultimate example of power! Each of them wields the strength of an entire army. To oppose any one of them, is to face your doom!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And Hazeel is one of them?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: More than that! Hazeel is one of the greatest Mahjarrat to have ever lived!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have no more questions.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Then we have nothing more to discuss until you've made your choice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I've made my decision.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll help you.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Good. Now, we are not the monsters that the Carnilleans would claim we are, so we were hoping to do this without violence. However, the fact they sent you here makes it clear that won't work now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Meaning?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: You must prove your loyalty to our cause, and Ceril Carnillean must die. Kill him for us, and we will know you are truly dedicated to Lord Hazeel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, how do I do it?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: I can already tell I was right about you. I can see you are of exactly the right character to join the followers of Hazeel.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Here, take this poison and pour it into Ceril Carnillean's food. Once the deed is done, return here and speak to me once more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some time to think on this.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Don't take too long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I won't help you.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Then you are a fool. Go back to your small-minded little life. You will never know the glories you could have tasted as one of us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know I'm going to stop you, right?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: You'll try, but you'll never find us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I need more time to think on this.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Don't take too long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some time to think on this.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Don't take too long.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: How did you get in here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've come for the Carnillean family armour. Hand it over, or face the consequences.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: I thought I made it clear to Jones that you could not be allowed to interfere with our mission. The incompetent fool must be going soft.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the butler's part of your little cult? Why is it always the butler? I should have known...

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Well you won't live long enough to tell anyone! Die!

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: You! Leave this place at once, before we're forced to make you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Hazeel Cultist
lines.text: You have no place here! Leave now, before someone makes you!

sections.title: Retrieving the Carnillean armour from the chest

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Get away from that chest!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Hello, adventurer. How are you doing with your quest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Drop the act, Jones. I know you're in league with the cultists.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Have you hit your head? That's a preposterous accusation!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? So you won't mind me telling Ceril then?

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Oh, you think he'll take your word over mine? He trusts me completely!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll see about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey! You owe me money.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: I owe you nothing you scoundrel! Now get out or I'll have Jones throw you out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: You! Don't think you can go around insulting our trusted staff and then waltz up to me as though nothing has happened!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Everything I said was the truth.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: Ha! Don't be so ridiculous! Next you'll be trying to tell me that Jones murdered our old butler!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Daddy says you don't like Mr Jones. Mr Jones is nice. He brings me toys and sweets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Jones is a bad man.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Well if you think that, I don't like you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'll do my best to console myself over your feelings towards me.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: I believe you've been asked to leave, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You won't get away with this, Jones! I know who you really are!

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: That's quite enough of that. All of us here are starting to tire of your theatrics.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This isn't over! I will find proof!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Hello. How are you today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not bad, thanks.

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Good good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello, adventurer. I hear you accused Jones of being part of that cult. Is that true?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is, but nobody believes me.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You have my utter faith, adventurer. I can't openly defy Sir Ceril, but I'd like you to know that I think you're on the right track with this.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I always found it suspicious that Jones would just show up on the doorstep the very day after the old butler died. Good luck in clearing your name

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: You're back. Have you had any luck yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look! I've recovered your armour!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Well done! I must say I am very impressed! Come on, hand it over.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Before we send you on your way with your payment, I'll just get Jones to whip you up a batch of his special broth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd rather not if it's all the same to you. Apparently Jones is in league with the cultists.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: What? Right! You! We're going to blooming well sort this out right now, once and for all!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Jones! This commoner says you had something to do with the cultists that stole my armour. What do you have to say for yourself?

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Nothing to do with me, sir. I am, as you know, a loyal servant.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Humph. Quite right too. I cannot fathom why this scoundrel would accuse you of such a crime without evidence to back up the accusations.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Right, I have made a decision. I have given my word as a nobleman to reward you for your efforts in retrieving my armour, but I must also compensate Jones for this terrible slander.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Now take this and leave! And don't darken my doorstep again!

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Don't worry, sir, he won't be bothering us any more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what do we have here...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ceril!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: What do you want now you scoundrel?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take a look at what I've found.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: What's this... Poison?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Jones?

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Just for killing rats, sir.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Right... but this amulet... I recognise this! Those cultists wore amulets like this!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Jones! We trusted you! We took you into our home!

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: You senile old fool... it was all too easy! I should have killed you and your pathetic family weeks ago!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Guard!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: This man is one of those cultists! Take him away!

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Don't think this is over, Ceril! You will die at the hands of the Hazeel Cult! I promise you that!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Well... It looks like I am indebted to you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem. We all make mistakes.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: But if it weren't for you... my whole family... we could have been...

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: I apologise for my harshness before. The very least I can do is to reward you for your noble efforts, and to offer my sincerest apologies as a gentleman.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: No, no, thank you! Feel free to stop by anytime, adventurer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Ceril.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Hello again, adventurer! It's good to see you! If it wasn't for your quick thinking, the treacherous Jones would have probably poisoned my family and I by now! We are in your debt.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry about it! A good deed is its own reward. And that money didn't hurt either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Well look who it is! Good to see you managed to clear your name. I always had faith in you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! So is everything all quiet around here now?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Indeed! For now at least, everything is back to normal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: What have you brought me? I want some more toys!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have any toys.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Toys! I want toys!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: Hello again, adventurer. Since you dealt with those horrible cultists, things have vastly improved for us around here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm happy to have helped.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Well hello there, adventurer! Are we fit and well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Glad to hear it.

sections.title: Obtaining the poison after an initially full inventory

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Here's that poison you need. Take it and pour it into Ceril Carnillean's food. Once the deed is done, return here and speak to me once more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: You have a mission for us, adventurer. Go to the Carnilliean household and poison Ceril Carnillean's meal to prove your loyalty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some more poison.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Fool! Be more careful with it this time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello there. I hear you're after that cult who broke in the other night. It always gladdens me when civilians assist the law like this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just happy to be of help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Hello, adventurer. I hear your quest is going well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Oh yes. Do keep up the good work.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: You're back. Have you had any luck yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid not, Ceril.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: That's Sir Ceril you blooming scoundrel! Show respect when addressing someone of my rank! I must say, I am a bit disapointed you haven't made any progress. Maybe I was wrong about you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, well, just unlucky I guess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: Oh, hello. I hope you've found those awful cultists! I simply cannot sleep at night knowing they're loose!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I haven't found them yet.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: Well you really are useless, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup, I guess I am.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Mommy said you're here to kill all the nasty people that keep breaking in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something like that.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Can I watch?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: You're that adventurer the family asked to help deal with those weird cultists, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's me.

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Good luck with that!

sections.title: Using the poison on the range
Talking to Claus after the poisoning

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Hello there. Caught any of those weird cultists yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Afraid not.

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Well, keep at it!

sections.title: Talking to Henryeta after the poisoning

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: Those hooligans! They slaughtered my precious Scruffy! I shall never recover! I am emotionally scarred for life!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... Hopefully someone finds them soon.

sections.title: Talking to Butler Jones after the poisoning

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Hello, adventurer. Such a terrible shame about Scruffy. I wonder if the family will ever fully recover.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Anyway, I hear your quest is going well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Oh yes. Do keep up the good work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Someone killed Scruffy. I liked Scruffy. He never told me off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... it's a real shame.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: I want my mommy.

sections.title: Talking to Ceril after the poisoning

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Oh the inhumanity... the cruelty... the misery... the pain...

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: My son is a good boy, really, but how could he give his dinner to Scruffy without having the servants test it for poison first? How? How could he be so thoughtless and careless? He knows we are all under threat!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Scruffy?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: He's been with our family for twenty years... that's 140 in dog years! The poor dog... What did he ever do to deserve such a fate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your dog got poisoned? That's not right.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: I agree! I hope whichever evildoer is responsible gets the full weight of the law brought upon them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... yeah... me too.

sections.title: Talking to Ceril again after the poisoning

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Oh the misery... How could someone do something like this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... I'm sure they won't get away with it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Today is a dark day. Those cultists have been back, and this time they've gone further than ever before! Murder! We can't afford to keep letting them get away with this.

sections.title: If the player did not have enough space for the amulet

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Here, you'll need this.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Going from left to right, turn each of the five sewer valves above so that they follow the design of the amulet. Once you've done that, you'll be able to use the raft here to enter our hideout.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I poisoned the food as requested, but it didn't quite go to plan.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Yes, we heard all about it from one of our sources. It's a shame that Ceril Carnillean survived, but that is no fault of yours. You have proven your loyalty to Hazeel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: I would like you to meet with our leader, Alomone. You'll find him in our hideout, deeper in the sewers. You can use this raft to get there.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: However, be warned that reaching the hideout is impossible unless the flow of water down here is set correctly. You'll need to use the sewer valves above to do that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how will I know how to set the valves?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: For that, you'll need this.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Going from left to right, turn each of the five sewer valves above so that they follow the design of the amulet. Once you've done that, you'll be able to use the raft here to enter our hideout.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost that amulet you gave me.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Careless! Look afer this one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Use the raft here to enter our hideout and meet with our leader, Alomone.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Be warned, you'll need to ensure the flow of water down here is set correctly for you to be able to use the raft.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Going from left to right, turn each of the five sewer valves above so that they follow the design of the amulet I gave you.

sections.title: Inspecting a sewer valve that is to the right
Inspecting a sewer valve that is to the left
Sucessfully using the raft with the correct water valve settings

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Hazeel Cultist
lines.text: I cannot talk right now. Lord Hazeel will soon be back with us and I have many preparations to make.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Well, well, well... we have a new recruit. Clivet told me of your desire to join us in our glorious task to resurrect the mighty Hazeel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Well if all goes well, you'll play a key role in just that. Before his defeat, Hazeel secured within his home a powerful magical scroll of restoration. It remains there still, hidden to even the foolish Carnilleans.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Up to now, our attempts to recover this scroll have been met with disappointment. However, I know you will not fail us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you want me to find this scroll?

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: That is correct. You must return to the Carnillean Mansion and find where it is hidden. We already have an agent in place to support you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Indeed. Their Butler Jones is in fact a faithful follower of Hazeel. He has been unsuccessful in locating the scroll himself, but together the two of you will find it.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Now, go with haste. Soon we will restore Hazeel to his true power and glory!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Alomone.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Hazeel has already waited far too long for his return. It is imperative that you find that scroll! It hides somewhere within the Carnillean Mansion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'm on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: It is good to see you once more, adventurer. Glory to Hazeel!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Ceril. How's things?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Sir Ceril! Sir! And things are terrible! Our beloved dog is dead, and I fear it's only a matter of time before those cultists strike again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'm sure it will all be okay in the end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: I just don't understand it! How? How could someone poison my delicious food without me even noticing? I... I just don't get it!

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Oh man... I hope I don't lose my job over this... but how? How did they do it? And why would they want to kill poor Scruffy anyway? He was such a good dog...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... it's a real mystery.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ceril. How are you?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Oh cruel world! Scruffy... I knew you well... I... just... don't think I can... go on without...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Loosen up already. It was only a DOG.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Hello, adventurer. I hear your quest is going well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm making progress. On that note, I hear you might be able to help me with my next task.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Ah yes, the scroll. I'm afraid I've searched high and low, but have so far been unable to locate it. I'm sure it is somewhere within the house, hidden where even the family won't find it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... So it must be somewhere they'd have never stepped foot. I'll keep on looking.

sections.title: Searching the crate in the kitchen
Searching a crate with the key
Searching the chest with a scroll already
Talking to Jones after obtaining the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Hello, adventurer. How is your quest going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm making progress.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Good. Remember, the scroll must be somewhere within the house, hidden where even the family won't find it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I'll keep on looking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the scroll!

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Then you should waste no time in returning to Alomone. The return of Hazeel will soon be upon us!

sections.title: Talking to Hentryeta after obtaining the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: I'm sorry. I am far too upset to talk to anybody right now. Oh... poor Scruffy...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... poor Scruffy.

sections.title: Talking to Ceril after obtaining the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Ceril. How's things?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Sir Ceril! Sir! And things are terrible! Our beloved dog is dead, and I fear it's only a matter of time before those cultists strike again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'm sure it will all be okay in the end.

sections.title: Talking to Philipe after obtaining the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Someone killed Scruffy. I liked Scruffy. He never told me off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... it's a real shame.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: I want my mommy.

sections.title: Talking to Claus after obtaining the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: I just don't understand it! How? How could someone poison my delicious food without me even noticing? I... I just don't get it!

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Oh man... I hope I don't lose my job over this... but how? How did they do it? And why would they want to kill poor Scruffy anyway? He was such a good dog...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... it's a real mystery.

sections.title: Talking to the guard after obtaining the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello, adventurer. I hope you find the cult soon. We have a horrible suspicion that there's been another burglary.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's worrying.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes, it is. The thing is, we can't even work out what they've taken! It's all very odd.

sections.title: Talking to Alomone after obtaining the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Alomone.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Have you brought the scroll of restoration?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it right here.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: At long last! With the words contained within this scroll, our lord shall finally return to us!

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Watch adventurer, and witness the glorious rebirth of Hazeel!

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Sentente sillaberi junque dithmento! Ia! Ia! Dextrimon encanto! Termando... Imcando... Solly enty rando... Sentente! Ia! Ia! Indenti zaggarati g'thxa!

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Dintenta! Sententa! Retenta! Q'exjta! Ia! Sottottott! Ia! Dysmenta junque fammatio svelken! Sottey! Sentey! Soloment!

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: My loyal followers, I have pride in you all. You have achieved a feat many would have considered impossible and returned me to this land.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Much time has passed and I have much to attend to, but first, I believe there is one here who deserves my gratitude.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer, I know that your efforts were essential in bringing about my return. You have my thanks. Know that I consider you an ally.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Now, I must leave you, loyal subjects, for time is short. My Mahjarrat kin will soon head northwards, and I have much to prepare.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Rest assured, when my work is done, I will return to you. Before long, the world will know of Hazeel once more!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, sorry.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: You must find it and bring it to me! Hazeel has already waited far too long for his return.
